mission.title: envoy: Support Body Transformations
mission.description: Support Body Transformations. Requested by 17+ users.

steps.title: Check current envoy deployment
steps.description:
Verify your envoy version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
helm list -n envoy 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working envoy installation.

steps.title: Review envoy configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant envoy configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get configmap -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=envoy
```

steps.title: Apply the fix for Support Body Transformations
steps.description:
Commit Message: http: new body transform filter with the substitution formatter
Additional Description:

To close https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/35783. There is enough context in the #35783. In short sentence, this PR add a transform filter to modify request or response headers by the substitution formatter API.

It's also possible to add body attributes to headers and refresh the routes.

Risk Level: low. New HTTP filter.
Testing: unit/integration.
Docs Changes: added.
Release Notes: Body transformation filter added.

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n envoy -l app.kubernetes.io/name=envoy
kubectl get events -n envoy --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Support Body Transformations" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
Commit Message: http: new body transform filter with the substitution formatter
Additional Description:

To close https://github.com/envoyproxy/envoy/issues/35783. There is enough context in the #35783. In short sentence, this PR add a transform filter to modify request or response headers by the substitution formatter API.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with envoy installed or the issue environment reproducible.
